title: melampyrum roseum subsp. hirsutum
intro: Melampyrum roseum subsp. hirsutum, also known as hairy dog's tooth, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. This hemiparasitic plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Melampyrum roseum subsp. hirsutum include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as asthma and bronchitis, as well as its ability to reduce fever and soothe digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Melampyrum roseum subsp. hirsutum, such as teas and infusions, can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
